records.category: Chapters on Legal Cases and Rulings
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Matters Related to Emancipation
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3565 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu al-Ward, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a Christian slave owned by a Muslim man-does the slave have to pay jizyah (tribute tax)?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, because his owner is the one responsible for redeeming him if he is taken, and he (the owner) must pay it on his behalf."
records.arabicText: 3565 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْوَرْدِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ مَمْلُوكٍ نَصْرَانِيٍّ لِرَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ عَلَيْهِ جِزْيَةٌ قَالَ "نَعَمْ إِنَّمَا هُوَ مَالِكُهُ يَفْتَدِيهِ إِذَا أُخِذَ يُؤَدِّي عَنْهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3565 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu al-Ward, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a Christian slave owned by a Muslim man-does the slave have to pay jizyah (tribute tax)?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, because his owner is the one responsible for redeeming him if he is taken, and he (the owner) must pay it on his behalf."

records.chapter: Chapter on Selling Milk Mixed with Water
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3981 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) forbade mixing milk with water for sale."
records.arabicText: 3981 - رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "نَهَى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَنْ يُشَابَ اَللَّبَنُ بِالْمَاءِ لِلْبَيْعِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3981 - Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) forbade mixing milk with water for sale."

records.chapter: Chapter on Defrauding the Unaware Buyer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3982 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Defrauding the one who entrusts you is unlawful gain, and defrauding a believer is forbidden."
records.arabicText: 3982 - قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "غَبْنُ اَلْمُسْتَرْسِلِ سُحْتٌ وَ غَبْنُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ حَرَامٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3982 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Defrauding the one who entrusts you is unlawful gain, and defrauding a believer is forbidden."

records.chapter: Chapter on Defrauding the Unaware Buyer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3983 - In a narration from Amr ibn Jameei, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"Defrauding the one who entrusts you is usury."
records.arabicText: 3983 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَمْرِو بْنِ جُمَيْعٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "غَبْنُ اَلْمُسْتَرْسِلِ رِبًا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3983 - In a narration from Amr ibn Jameei, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Defrauding the one who entrusts you is usury."

records.chapter: Chapter on Defrauding the Unaware Buyer
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3984 - Imam (as) said: "If a man says to another man, 'Come, I will improve your sale,' then it is forbidden for him to make a profit."
[REFERENCES]
Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.152 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.272 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.7 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.457 • Wasa'il Al-Shi'ah, Vol.17 p.395 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.136
records.arabicText: 3984 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِذَا قَالَ اَلرَّجُلُ لِلرَّجُلِ هَلُمَّ أَحْسِنْ بَيْعَكَ فَقَدْ حَرُمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلرِّبْحُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3984 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a man says to another man, 'Come, I will improve your sale,' then it is forbidden for him to make a profit."
[REFERENCES]
Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.152 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.272 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.7 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.457 • Wasa'il Al-Shi'ah, Vol.17 p.395 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.136

records.chapter: Chapter on Kindness and Avoiding Deception in Sales
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3985 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said to Zaynab, the perfume seller, known as Al-Hawla': "When you sell, do so with excellence and do not deceive, for it is purer and more enduring for wealth."
records.arabicText: 3985 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ، لِزَيْنَبَ اَلْعَطَّارَةِ اَلْحَوْلاَءِ "إِذَا بِعْتِ فَأَحْسِنِي وَ لاَ تَغُشِّي فَإِنَّهُ أَنْقَى وَ أَبْقَى لِلْمَالِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3985 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said to Zaynab, the perfume seller, known as Al-Hawla':
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When you sell, do so with excellence and do not deceive, for it is purer and more enduring for wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Kindness and Avoiding Deception in Sales
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3986 - Imam (as) said: "He who deceives a Muslim is not from us."
records.arabicText: 3986 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَيْسَ مِنَّا مَنْ غَشَّ مُسْلِماً ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Kindness and Avoiding Deception in Sales
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3987 - Imam (as) said: "Whoever deceives the Muslims will be resurrected with the Jews on the Day of Judgment, because they are the most deceitful people towards the Muslims."
records.arabicText: 3987 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ غَشَّ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ حُشِرَ مَعَ اَلْيَهُودِ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ لِأَنَّهُمْ أَغَشُّ اَلنَّاسِ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3987 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever deceives the Muslims will be resurrected with the Jews on the Day of Judgment, because they are the most deceitful people towards the Muslims."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3988 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said: "None of you should intercept food outside the city, and no resident should sell on behalf of a visitor. Leave the Muslims so that Allah (swt) may provide for some of them through others."
records.arabicText: 3988 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "لاَ يَتَلَقَّى أَحَدُكُمْ طَعَاماً خَارِجاً مِنَ اَلْمِصْرِ وَ لاَ يَبِيعُ حَاضِرٌ لِبَادٍ ذَرُوا اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ يَرْزُقِ اَللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3988 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "None of you should intercept food outside the city, and no resident should sell on behalf of a visitor. Leave the Muslims so that Allah (swt) may provide for some of them through others."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3989 - It is narrated from Minhal al-Qassab who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about intercepting sheep.
Imam (as) said: "Do not intercept, do not buy what has been intercepted, and do not eat the meat of what has been intercepted."
records.arabicText: 3989 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مِنْهَالٍ اَلْقَصَّابِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ تَلَقِّي اَلْغَنَمِ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَلَقَّ وَ لاَ تَشْتَرِ مَا تُلُقِّيَ وَ لاَ تَأْكُلْ مِنْ لَحْمِ مَا تُلُقِّيَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3989 - It is narrated from Minhal al-Qassab who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about intercepting sheep.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not intercept, do not buy what has been intercepted, and do not eat the meat of what has been intercepted."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3990 - It is narrated: "The limit for intercepting is the distance of a short journey, but if it reaches four farsakhs, then it is considered transportation."
records.arabicText: 3990 - وَ رُوِيَ : "أَنَّ حَدَّ اَلتَّلَقِّي رَوْحَةٌ فَإِذَا صَارَ إِلَى أَرْبَعِ فَرَاسِخَ فَهُوَ جَلْبٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3990 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The limit for intercepting is the distance of a short journey, but if it reaches four farsakhs, then it is considered transportation."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3991 - Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "One dirham of usury is more severe in the sight of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, than thirty acts of fornication, all of which are committed with forbidden relatives, such as a maternal aunt or a paternal aunt."
records.arabicText: 3991 - رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ اَلْمُخْتَارِ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "دِرْهَمٌ رِبًا أَشَدُّ عِنْدَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ ثَلاَثِينَ زَنْيَةً كُلُّهَا بِذَاتِ مَحْرَمٍ مِثْلِ اَلْخَالَةِ وَ اَلْعَمَّةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3991 - Al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One dirham of usury is more severe in the sight of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic, than thirty acts of fornication, all of which are committed with forbidden relatives, such as a maternal aunt or a paternal aunt."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3992 - In a narration from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
"One dirham of usury is more severe in the sight of Allah (swt) than seventy acts of fornication, all of which are committed with forbidden relatives."
records.arabicText: 3992 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "دِرْهَمٌ رِبًا أَشَدُّ عِنْدَ اَللَّهِ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ زَنْيَةً كُلِّهَا بِذَاتِ مَحْرَمٍ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3992 - In a narration from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Abdullah (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One dirham of usury is more severe in the sight of Allah (swt) than seventy acts of fornication, all of which are committed with forbidden relatives."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3993 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said:
"The one who consumes usury, the one who gives it, its recorder, and its two witnesses are equal in sin."
records.arabicText: 3993 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "آكِلُ اَلرِّبَا وَ مُؤْكِلُهُ وَ كَاتِبُهُ وَ شَاهِدَاهُ فِي اَلْوِزْرِ سَوَاءٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3993 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The one who consumes usury, the one who gives it, its recorder, and its two witnesses are equal in sin."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3994 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) cursed usury, its consumer, its giver, its seller, its buyer, its recorder, and its two witnesses."
records.arabicText: 3994 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَعَنَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلرِّبَا وَ آكِلَهُ وَ مُؤْكِلَهُ وَ بَائِعَهُ وَ مُشْتَرِيَهُ وَ كَاتِبَهُ وَ شَاهِدَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3994 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) cursed usury, its consumer, its giver, its seller, its buyer, its recorder, and its two witnesses."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3995 - Ibrahim ibn Umar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: "And whatever you give as usury so that it may increase in the wealth of people, it does not increase with Allah (swt)" (Surah Ar-Rum 30:39)
Imam (as) said: "It is your gift to a man through which you seek a better return than it, and that is consumed as usury."
records.arabicText:
3995 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: ۝ وَ مٰا آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ رِباً لِيَرْبُوَا فِي أَمْوٰالِ اَلنّٰاسِ فَلاٰ يَرْبُوا عِنْدَ اَللّٰهِ ۝
قَالَ "هُوَ هَدِيَّتُكَ إِلَى اَلرَّجُلِ تَطْلُبُ مِنْهُ اَلثَّوَابَ أَفْضَلَ مِنْهَا فَذَلِكَ رِبًا يُؤْكَلُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3995 - Ibrahim ibn Umar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding the saying of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Majestic: "And whatever you give as usury so that it may increase in the wealth of people, it does not increase with Allah (swt)" (Surah Ar-Rum 30:39)
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is your gift to a man through which you seek a better return than it, and that is consumed as usury."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3996 - Ubayd ibn Zurarah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "Usury only applies to what is measured or weighed."
records.arabicText: 3996 - وَ رَوَى عُبَيْدُ بْنُ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ يَكُونُ اَلرِّبَا إِلاَّ فِيمَا يُكَالُ أَوْ يُوزَنُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3996 - Ubayd ibn Zurarah narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Usury only applies to what is measured or weighed."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3997 - Imam (as) said: "Any usury that people consumed out of ignorance, and then they repented, it will be accepted from them once their repentance is recognized."
And Imam (as) said: "If a man inherits wealth from his father and knows that there is usury in that wealth, but it has been mixed with other lawful trade, then it is permissible and pure for him to consume it. However, if he identifies a specific portion as usury, he should take his principal amount and return the usury."
records.arabicText: 3997 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلُّ رِبًا أَكَلَهُ اَلنَّاسُ بِجَهَالَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابُوا فَإِنَّهُ يُقْبَلُ مِنْهُمْ إِذَا عُرِفَتْ مِنْهُمُ اَلتَّوْبَةُ " وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً وَرِثَ مِنْ أَبِيهِ مَالاً وَ قَدْ عَلِمَ أَنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ اَلْمَالِ رِبًا وَ لَكِنْ قَدِ اِخْتَلَطَ فِي اَلتِّجَارَةِ بِغَيْرِهِ فَإِنَّهُ لَهُ حَلاَلٌ طَيِّبٌ فَلْيَأْكُلْهُ وَ إِنْ عَرَفَ مِنْهُ شَيْئاً مَعْزُولاً أَنَّهُ رِبًا فَلْيَأْخُذْ رَأْسَ مَالِهِ وَ لْيَرُدَّ اَلرِّبَا ".

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3998 - Imam (as) said:
"If any man manages a large amount of wealth and has excessively engaged in usury unknowingly, then later becomes aware of it and wishes to remove it, what has already passed is permissible for him, but he must refrain from it in the future."
records.arabicText:
3998 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"أَيُّمَا رَجُلٍ أَدَارَ مَالاً كَثِيراً قَدْ أَكْثَرَ فِيهِ مِنَ اَلرِّبَا فَجَهِلَ ذَلِكَ ثُمَّ عَرَفَهُ بَعْدُ فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يَنْزِعَ ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ فَمَا مَضَى فَلَهُ وَ يَدَعُهُ فِيمَا يَسْتَأْنِفُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3998 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If any man manages a large amount of wealth and has excessively engaged in usury unknowingly, then later becomes aware of it and wishes to remove it, what has already passed is permissible for him, but he must refrain from it in the future."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3999 - Imam (as) said:
"A man came to Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) and said: "I have inherited wealth, and I know that the one from whom I inherited it used to engage in usury. I am certain that there is usury in it, and this knowledge makes even the lawful portion unpleasant for me. I have asked the jurists of Iraq and Hijaz, and they said it is not permissible for me to consume it because of what it contains."
Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said to him:
"If you know that there is a specific portion of it that is usury and you know its rightful owners, then take your principal amount and return what is beyond that. But if it is mixed, then consume it with ease and satisfaction, for the wealth is yours. Avoid what its previous owner used to do. Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) forgave what has passed of usury and prohibited what remains.
So whoever was ignorant of it, his ignorance is excused until he becomes aware of it. Once he knows it is prohibited, it becomes forbidden for him, and punishment becomes obligatory upon him if he engages in it, just as it is obligatory upon one who consumes usury."
records.arabicText:
3999 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"أَتَى رَجُلٌ إِلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ إِنِّي وَرِثْتُ مَالاً وَ قَدْ عَلِمْتُ أَنَّ صَاحِبَهُ اَلَّذِي وَرِثْتُهُ مِنْهُ قَدْ كَانَ يُرْبِي وَ قَدْ أَعْرِفُ أَنَّ فِيهِ رِبًا وَ أَسْتَيْقِنُ ذَلِكَ وَ لَيْسَ يَطِيبُ لِي حَلاَلُهُ لِحَالِ عِلْمِي فِيهِ وَ قَدْ سَأَلْتُ فُقَهَاءَ أَهْلِ اَلْعِرَاقِ وَ أَهْلِ اَلْحِجَازِ فَقَالُوا لاَ يَحِلُّ لَكَ أَكْلُهُ مِنْ أَجْلِ مَا فِيهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "إِنْ كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ فِيهِ مَالاً مَعْرُوفاً رِبًا وَ تَعْرِفُ أَهْلَهُ فَخُذْ رَأْسَ مَالِكَ وَ رُدَّ مَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مُخْتَلِطاً فَكُلْهُ هَنِيئاً مَرِيئاً فَإِنَّ اَلْمَالَ مَالُكَ وَ اِجْتَنِبْ مَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ صَاحِبُهُ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَدْ وَضَعَ مَا مَضَى مِنَ اَلرِّبَا وَ حَرَّمَ مَا بَقِيَ فَمَنْ جَهِلَهُ وَسِعَهُ جَهْلُهُ حَتَّى يَعْرِفَهُ فَإِذَا عَرَفَ تَحْرِيمَهُ حَرُمَ عَلَيْهِ وَ وَجَبَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ اَلْعُقُوبَةُ إِذَا رَكِبَهُ كَمَا يَجِبُ عَلَى مَنْ يَأْكُلُ اَلرِّبَا" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3999 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A man came to Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) and said: "I have inherited wealth, and I know that the one from whom I inherited it used to engage in usury. I am certain that there is usury in it, and this knowledge makes even the lawful portion unpleasant for me. I have asked the jurists of Iraq and Hijaz, and they said it is not permissible for me to consume it because of what it contains."
Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said to him:
"If you know that there is a specific portion of it that is usury and you know its rightful owners, then take your principal amount and return what is beyond that. But if it is mixed, then consume it with ease and satisfaction, for the wealth is yours. Avoid what its previous owner used to do. Indeed, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) forgave what has passed of usury and prohibited what remains.
So whoever was ignorant of it, his ignorance is excused until he becomes aware of it. Once he knows it is prohibited, it becomes forbidden for him, and punishment becomes obligatory upon him if he engages in it, just as it is obligatory upon one who consumes usury."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4000 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said: "There is no usury between us and the people with whom we are at war; we take from them, but we do not give to them."
records.arabicText: 4000 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "لَيْسَ بَيْنَنَا وَ بَيْنَ أَهْلِ حَرْبِنَا رِبًا نَأْخُذُ مِنْهُمْ وَ لاَ نُعْطِيهِمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4000 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (may Allah (swt) bless him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no usury between us and the people with whom we are at war; we take from them, but we do not give to them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4001 - Imam (as) said: "There is no usury between a man and his child, and there is no usury between a master and his servant."
records.arabicText: 4001 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَيْسَ بَيْنَ اَلرَّجُلِ وَ بَيْنَ وَلَدِهِ رِبًا وَ لَيْسَ بَيْنَ اَلسَّيِّدِ وَ بَيْنَ عَبْدِهِ رِبًا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4001 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no usury between a man and his child, and there is no usury between a master and his servant."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4002 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There is no usury between a Muslim and a Dhimmi, nor between a woman and her husband."
records.arabicText: 4002 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَيْسَ بَيْنَ اَلْمُسْلِمِ وَ بَيْنَ اَلذِّمِّيِّ رِبًا وَ لاَ بَيْنَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ وَ بَيْنَ زَوْجِهَا رِبًا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4002 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no usury between a Muslim and a Dhimmi, nor between a woman and her husband."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4003 - It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, the seller of Sabiri, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "May I be your ransom! People claim that making a profit from someone in distress is forbidden and is considered usury."
Imam (as) said: "Have you ever seen anyone, whether rich or poor, buy something except out of necessity, O Umar? Indeed, 'Allah (swt) has permitted trade and forbidden usury.' So make a profit, but do not commit usury."
I said: "What is usury?"
Imam (as) said: "It is exchanging dirhams for dirhams of equal amount."
records.arabicText:
4003 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ بَيَّاعِ اَلسَّابِرِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنَّ اَلنَّاسَ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّ اَلرِّبْحَ عَلَى اَلْمُضْطَرِّ حَرَامٌ وَ هُوَ مِنَ اَلرِّبَا فَقَالَ
"وَ هَلْ رَأَيْتَ أَحَداً اِشْتَرَى غَنِيّاً أَوْ فَقِيراً إِلاَّ مِنْ ضَرُورَةٍ يَا عُمَرُ قَدْ "أَحَلَّ اَللّٰهُ اَلْبَيْعَ وَ حَرَّمَ اَلرِّبٰا" فَارْبَحْ وَ لاَ تُرْبِهِ " قُلْتُ وَ مَا اَلرِّبَا قَالَ "دَرَاهِمُ بِدَرَاهِمَ مِثْلاَنِ بِمِثْلٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4003 - It is narrated from Umar ibn Yazid, the seller of Sabiri, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"May I be your ransom! People claim that making a profit from someone in distress is forbidden and is considered usury."
Imam (as) said: "Have you ever seen anyone, whether rich or poor, buy something except out of necessity, O Umar? Indeed, 'Allah (swt) has permitted trade and forbidden usury.' So make a profit, but do not commit usury."
I said: "What is usury?"
Imam (as) said: "It is exchanging dirhams for dirhams of equal amount."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4004 - Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) disliked the sale of meat in exchange for live animals.
records.arabicText: 4004 - وَ رَوَى غِيَاثُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَرِهَ بَيْعَ اَللَّحْمِ بِالْحَيَوَانِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4004 - Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) disliked the sale of meat in exchange for live animals.
